post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 186: Experimental Log of the Crazy Lich Mafia
Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not refer to private conversations with the moderator in any way unless given permission.
    • 1.5: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.7: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.8: To verify that you are a participant in this game, post the next paragraph from the Experimental Log of the Crazy Lich in bold. The first person to do so must post the second paragraph of the first chapter, the second person must post the third paragraph, etc.
    • 1.9: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.10: Do advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.11: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.10: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.11: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 3.5: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.6: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.7: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline will not be counted.
    • 3.8: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.11: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.12: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.13: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 186 and Charge is the co-moderator.
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Reading the book up to chapter 26 is highly recommended, and you should also read the rest of the book as well, as it is is very entertaining.

Sulfur Mountain City is a bastion of peace and stability within the chaotic Underground World. The city is governed by strict laws enforced by the ever vigilant Town Security. the city is a shining example of equality, a place where no race is discriminated against. Indeed, the city is a model for Order in the Underground World. The three Heads of the City, the Sword Saint Adam Han, the Great Saint Margaret Layde, and the Red Dragon Ein Mezus, are illustrious heroes who rose to prominence saving the world from the calamity of Eternal Night. Under their protection, the city has flourished.

However, the city heads have left the city to investigate a disturbance taking place in the volcano the city was built over. Normally, their absence would only be of minor concern. However, a group of dark elves from Morsblight City, an evil place under the control of the elf goddess Lorci, a viscous and cunning individual who schemes to gain power, have arrived in the city under the guise of a diplomatic delegation. Soon after, mysterious deaths have begun occurring. During this time of crisis, the people of Sulfur Mountain City must defend their homes from those who seek to subvert it.


Do not post until the first line of the book has been posted below by me.

Day 1 begins. Current time: 15:00:00 EDT 5 June 2019.

post_rendered:
Usually co-moderators don't do this.
However, I am nice...

Highlights in Rules and Setup
* Do not quote any PMs. Do not refer to private conversations with the moderator in any way unless given permission
* The capitalization of the player name does not matter [when voting].
* If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged.
* If at the end of the day, no player has the majority of the votes, no one will be hanged.
* During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
* If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced.
* If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.

See, what you don't understand is why I'm playing this way. There's a whole backstory.
Once upon a time, I had pet kitten named kittenwarrior. But there was also this big mean dog who bullied her. He would always steal her food in the middle of the night. The problem was, all my neighbors had big mean dogs, so I never knew which big mean dog was bullying my poor kitten. So I tried talking to each of the dogs, hoping one of them would slip up and give away that it was him... but all of them refused to talk to me, except one dog named owl. However, as I was talking to owl, I quickly became aware that this was not a conversation. This was a one-sided rant in which owl was aggressively trying to flip the situation and accuse me of stealing my own kitten's food! Frustrated, I raised my fist and knocked this silly dog on the head. His head jerked back, and as he exposed his neck, I noticed a pedant! Shaped identically to the dent the bully had left on the food bowl! And thus, I had caught the offender. All was well until kitten's food was stolen the next day again. It was then that I realised there may be multiple offenders...

post_rendered:
thepiercingarrow wrote:
Why isn't it allowed?

The utilization of the technique in question necessarily involves the delivery of information to an outside source configured by a player in the game, and since the delivery of such information results in feedback from an outside source, there are grounds for it to be classified as communication. The asynchronous nature of such communication does not preclude it from being an activity prohibited under both rule 1.5 and forum rule 7 since the medium of communication is unauthorized. In essence, the behavior being prohibited can effectively be described as communication with an entity created by another player through an unauthorized medium, thus making it a violation of the rules. Furthermore, the effect of such an activity is such as to enable a transfer of information between two players which cannot be understood by those who can see such a message, and the two players in question need not necessarily share any relation that should facilitate the transfer of any information. While such an action is not explicitly prohibited by the rules, it is certainly against the intent laid forth when the rules were written. Furthermore, the impact of allowing such an action is such as to generate an effect with tangible and significant effects on the game, in a manner not intended when the game was created. From a broader perspective, the action is both in violation of the letter of the law and the spirit, and has a nonnegligible effect on the progression of the game, thus it is only proper for it to be prohibited.

post_rendered:
It has come to my attention that I have violated the spirit, if not the letter, of activity rules. Along with this the unwritten code of good conduct between players requiring a basic minimum of activity. I have failed to align myself with these standards, and while it is not altogether my fault, I grossly miscalculated the amount of time I would be spending this summer doing things not AoPS-related, especially not mafia-related. It has become apparent that my time this summer is very small, and what free time I would have (which is variable and I'm not sure how much it is) would be much better spent doing other things. bandwagoning is when you just agree with what someone says

If at any time, a majority of the votes are for innocent, then the player will be let go. Then, a new player can be nominated to be put on trial. The judge has access to a vote worth 4. The players roles will only be revealed at the end of the day. Once three trials have concluded, or the deadline passes, the day will end.


Vote by posting either Vote: Guilty or Vote: Innocent.

Vote: Guilty

My logic here is based on the fact that if the Mafia control the judge, we've already lost, barring PRs. So, I'm assuming it's town controlled. That means that we can discuss who the judge should pick for each trial. I think that kitten/ben is v/v or w/w, leaning towards w/w because if they are v/v then one of chess/mathjay/madelyn is scum and I don't think that is the case. So, we can kill kitten, and if kitten is scum kill ben and then SmartZX and that's three likely mafia, with MY-2 as tomorrows hang for the near guaranteed win. If kitten is town, we can still kill SmartZX and then no hang the third trial, because if SmartZX is scum that gives us 5 players tomorrow and probably 2 mafia.

The other idea I had was just no hanging today but I think we're far enough behind that it's not a winning strategy. Assuming there are 3 mafia, we would need 3 correct hangs in a row which is not likely. This would get a lot better if there is a PR that has info clearing one or more players, so I think a claim would make sense if there is such a PR right now.

Thoughts?

FMPOV Town Security is not anti-town but is anti-me, and therefore FMPOV equally worth hanging as scum

Attached below is the game document that contains all Role PMs and night actions.


About the balance of the game:
Town did have several very strong roles. However, there was a lot of synergy involved. Death of a PR can reduce the power of other PRs. For example, if either chess or MY died, the kill would be lost. Also, losing Gentlemen means that the Judge has access to less confirmed towns. However, in this game basically all the PRs survived unscathed by Day 3, which made Roland very powerful (though ben failed to utilize it properly). Had any of the Gentlemen died, it would have gone quite differently.

Moreover, the town was divided as multiple factions were at odds at each other, making it so that town could not operate as a single entity. (In fact, had MY not submitted a kill on mathjay, and kitten was convinced to hang ben, MY did have the option to side with mafia temporarily to eliminate the gentlemen, hoping to then turn to hang mathjay. Such possible scenarios mean that mafia has some additional advantages.

Overall, mafia suffered pretty bad luck this game, which hurt their chances.
